instruction:
The Question Paper contains three sections.
 Section A has 25 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section B has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section C has 6 questions. Attempt any 5 questions.
 All questions carry equal marks.
 There is no negative marking.

questions.question: Mendeleev proposed that some of the elements were still undiscovered and, therefore, left several gaps in the table. He left the gap under aluminium and a gap under silicon and called these elements Eka-Aluminium and Eka-Silicon. These were later discovered and named.
questions.question_html: Mendeleev proposed that some of the elements were still undiscovered and, therefore, left several gaps in the table. He left the gap under aluminium and a gap under silicon and called these elements Eka-Aluminium and Eka-Silicon. These were later discovered and named.
questions.explanation: Mendeleev arranged the elements according to their atomic weights. He corrected the atomic weights of a few existing elements to explain their chemical and physical behaviour. Moreover, he felt that few elements are still to be discovered, so he left empty spaces for them in his periodic table. Two such elements whose properties he predicted; and were later discovered are Gallium and Germanium respectively.

questions.question: The period number in the long form of the periodic table is equal to:
questions.question_html: The period number in the long form of the periodic table is equal to:

options.text: maximum Principal quantum number of any element of the period.
options.html: maximum Principal quantum number of any element of the period.

options.text: atomic number of any element of the period.
options.html: atomic number of any element of the period.

options.text: maximum Azimuthal quantum number of any element of the period.
options.html: maximum Azimuthal quantum number of any element of the period.

options.text: magnetic quantum number of any element of the period.
options.html: magnetic quantum number of any element of the period.

correct_options: maximum Principal quantum number of any element of the period.
questions.explanation: The principal quantum number is represented by 'n'. it determines the periods of the element e.g Mg maximum principal quantum no. is 3 thus it belong to period 3 of the periodic table.

questions.question: Which of the following oxides is neutral?
questions.question_html: Which of the following oxides is neutral?
questions.explanation: CO(carbon monoxide) has a triple covalent bound between C and O and therefore no ionization of any kind. The covalent character of carbon means that there is no tendency for carbon to ionize on its own to form C^(4+) or C^(4-), hence CO stays as neutral oxide.

questions.question: Oxidation and reduction always occur simultaneously, hence, the word “redox” was coined for this class of chemical reactions. In the following redox reaction, identify the species undergoing oxidation and reduction: 3Fe_(3)O_(4)(s) + 8 Al (s) $\longrightarrow$ 9 Fe (s) + 4Al_(2)O_(3)(s)
questions.question_html: Oxidation and reduction always occur simultaneously, hence, the word “redox” was coined for this class of chemical reactions. In the following redox reaction, identify the species undergoing oxidation and reduction: 3Fe3O4(s) + 8 Al (s) {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} 9 Fe (s) + 4Al2O3(s)

options.text: Al_(2)O_(3) is reduced, Fe_(3)O_(4) is oxidised
options.html: Al<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub> is reduced, Fe<sub>3</sub>O<sub>4</sub> is oxidised

options.text: Fe_(3)O_(4) is oxidised, Fe is reduced
options.html: Fe<sub>3</sub>O<sub>4</sub> is oxidised, Fe is reduced

options.text: Fe_(3)O_(4) is oxidised, Aluminium is reduced
options.html: Fe<sub>3</sub>O<sub>4</sub> is oxidised, Aluminium is reduced

questions.explanation: In the above reaction, oxygen is transferred from Fe_(3)O_(4) to Al, so Fe_(3)O_(4) is reduced while Al is oxidised. As per the definition of oxidation and reduction, loss of oxygen i.e reduction occurs in Fe_(3)O_(4), and gain of oxygen i.e Oxidation occurs in Al.

questions.question: Hydrogen peroxide is used as:
questions.question_html: Hydrogen peroxide is used as:
questions.explanation:
Hydrogen peroxide (H_(2)O_(2)) is a very pale blue liquid that appears colourless in a dilute solution, slightly more viscous than water. It is a weak acid. It has strong oxidizing properties and is, therefore, a powerful bleaching agent that is mostly used for bleaching paper but has also found use as a disinfectant and as an oxidizer.

Hydrogen peroxide in acid solution is oxidized with KMnO_(4) and reduced with KI. When H_(2)O_(2) serves as an oxidizing agent, the oxygen is reduced to H_(2)O. When H_(2)O_(2) serves as a reducing agent, the oxygen is oxidized to O_(2) and bubbles are noticed.

questions.question:
The reaction

 $C{H_3}CH = C{H_2} + HBr\xrightarrow{Peroxide} C{H_3}C{H_2}C{H_2}Br$ can be explained by
questions.question_html:
The reaction
{tex}C{H_3}CH = C{H_2} + HBr\xrightarrow{Peroxide} C{H_3}C{H_2}C{H_2}Br{/tex}  can be explained by
questions.explanation: In the presence of peroxide and light, the addition of HBr to unsymmetrical alkenes occurs contrary to Markovnikov's rule. The chemistry follows a free-radical mechanism. Organic reactions, which proceed by homolytic fission are called free radical or homopolar or nonpolar reactions.

questions.question: Fill up the blanks with a suitable word in the statement, " Physical properties are those properties which are measured or observed ________ changing the identity or the composition of the substance ."
questions.question_html: Fill up the  blanks  with a suitable word in the statement, " Physical properties are those properties which are measured or observed ________ changing the identity or the composition of the substance ."
questions.explanation: A substance is identified by its composition, hence for measuring physical properties of a substance its identity must be retained, (or should not be changed) Hence, the given statement, " Physical properties are those properties which are measured or observed without changing the identity or composition of the substance." is correct.

questions.question: The typical range of molar enthalpies for the strongest intermolecular (Hydrogen) bonds is:
questions.question_html: The typical range of molar enthalpies for the strongest intermolecular (Hydrogen) bonds is:
questions.explanation: A hydrogen bond is an electrostatic attraction between two polar groups that occurs when a hydrogen (H) atom linked to a small-sized highly electronegative atom such as nitrogen (N), oxygen (O), or fluorine (F). Therefore unlike a covalent bond, it has very low dissociation energy with a range of molar enthalpy = (4 - 25) kJ.

questions.question: In the Lassaigne's test for nitrogen in an organic compound, the Prussian blue colour is obtained due to the formation of :
questions.question_html: In the Lassaigne's test for nitrogen in an organic compound, the Prussian blue colour is obtained due to the formation of :
questions.explanation: In Lassaigne's test, the sodium fusion extract is boiled with iron(II) sulphate and then acidified with concentrated sulphuric acid. The formation of Prussian blue colour confirms the presence of nitrogen. Sodium cyanide first reacts with iron(II)sulphate and forms sodium hexacyanoferrate(II). On heating with concentrated sulphuric acid some iron(II) ions are oxidised to iron(III) ions which react with sodium hexacyanoferrate(II) to produce iron(III) hexacyanoferrate(II) (ferriferrocyanide) which is Prussian blue in colour. Thus the prussion blue colour is due the formation of Fe_(4)[Fe(CN)_(6)]_(3)

questions.question: Which of the following attain the linear structure?
questions.question_html: Which of the following attain the linear structure?
questions.explanation:
Molecular geometry is determined by the hybridization of the central atom. In CS_(2) the carbon atom is surrounded by two regions of electron density, one for each double bond it forms with the sulfur atoms. The molecular geometry will thus be linear, the basic AX_(2) model.

 ![image](https://media-mycbseguide.s3.amazonaws.com/images/cbse/review/ques_21466.png) 

The molecular geometry of BeCl_(2) is linear with symmetric charge distribution around the central atom.

 ![image](https://media-mycbseguide.s3.amazonaws.com/images/cbse/review/ques_21466_1.png)

questions.question: Aurous chloride and auric chloride are written as Au(l)Cl and Au(lll)Cl_(3). The roman numeral in parenthesis after the symbol of the metal in the molecular formula represents the:
questions.question_html: Aurous chloride and auric chloride are written as Au(l)Cl and Au(lll)Cl3. The roman numeral in parenthesis after the symbol of the metal in the molecular formula represents the:
questions.explanation: The roman number represents the oxidation number/oxidation state of an element. It is popularly known as Stock notation given by the german scientist Alfred stock.

questions.question: Which of the following equations depict the oxidising nature of H_(2)O_(2)?
questions.question_html: Which of the following equations depict the oxidising nature of H2O2?
questions.explanation: I^(-) gets oxidize to I_(2) in presence H_(2)O_(2) which itself get reduce to H_(2)O.

questions.question: Which of the following is an electrophile?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is an electrophile?
questions.explanation: A reagent that takes away an electron pair is called electrophile (E+) i.e., electron seeking. In neutral molecules such as alkyl halides, due to the polarity of the C-X bond a partial positive charge is generated on the carbon atom and hence the carbon atom becomes an electrophilic centre at which a nucleophile can attack. Dimethyl chloride is thus an electrophile.

questions.question: What will be the volume of CO_(2) at NTP obtained on heating 10 grams of (90% pure) limestone
questions.question_html: What will be the volume of CO2 at NTP obtained on heating 10 grams of (90% pure) limestone
questions.explanation: 2.016 litre

questions.question: The de Broglie wavelength of a tennis ball of mass 60 g moving with a velocity of 10 metres per second is approximately:
questions.question_html: The de Broglie wavelength of a tennis ball of mass 60 g moving with a velocity of 10 metres per second is approximately:
questions.explanation:
The mass of the ball = 60 g = 6 $\times$ 10^(-2) kg

Velocity of the ball = 10m/s

let the de broglie wavelength be $\lambda $ .

We know, $\lambda={h\over mv}$ 

 $\lambda ={6.626\times 10^{-34}\over6\times 10^{-2}\times 10}$ = $1.103\times 10^{-33}\approx 10^{-33}m$

questions.question: Oxidation number denotes the oxidation state of an element in a compound ascertained on the basis that electron in a covalent bond belongs
questions.question_html: Oxidation number denotes the oxidation state of an element in a compound ascertained on the basis that electron in a covalent bond belongs

options.text: entirely to transition elements if present
options.html: entirely to transition elements if present

options.text: entirely to halogens in a compound
options.html: entirely to halogens in a compound

questions.explanation:
Oxidation number is a number assigned to an element in a compound according to some rules. This number enable us to describe oxidation-reduction reactions, and balancing redox chemical reactions. When a covalent bond forms between two atoms with different electronegativities the shared electrons in the bond lie closer to the more electronegative atom eg. HCl

The oxidation number of an atom is the charge that results when the electrons in a covalent bond are assigned to the more electronegative atom. It is the charge on an atom would possess if the bonding were ionic. In HCl (above) the oxidation number for the hydrogen would be +1 and that of the Cl would be -1

questions.question: For a stable molecule the value of bond order must be:
questions.question_html: For a stable molecule the value of bond order must be:

options.text: there is no relationship between stability and bond order
options.html: there is no relationship between stability and bond order

questions.explanation:
Bond order $\propto $ Stability

Hence, for a stable molecule, the value of bond order must be positive. When bond order is zero and negative the molecule will not exist.

questions.question: Which of the following carbocation is most stable?
questions.question_html: Which of the following carbocation is most stable?
questions.explanation: ${(C{H_3})_3}\mathop C\limits^ \oplus $ i.e. the tertiary carbocation is most stable. Alkyl groups directly attached to the positively charged carbon stabilise the carbocations due to inductive and hyperconjugation effects.

 Student

 
 
 Readings

 
 
 
 
 A

 B

questions.question_html:
Two students performed the same experiment separately and each one of them recorded two readings of mass which are given below. Correct reading of mass is 3.0 g. On the basis of given data, mark the correct option out of the following statements.

Student

Readings

A

3.01

2.99

B

3.05

2.95

options.text: Results of student B are neither precise nor accurate.
options.html: Results of student B are neither precise nor accurate.

options.text: Results of both the students are neither accurate nor precise.
options.html: Results of both the students are neither accurate nor precise.

options.text: Results of student A are both precise and accurate.
options.html: Results of student A are both precise and accurate.

options.text: Results of student B are both precise and accurate.
options.html: Results of student B are both precise and accurate.

correct_options: Results of student A are both precise and accurate.
questions.explanation: The average of readings of student A and B is 3.00. But the readings of student A are close to each other as well as close to average value as compare to values of student B. Hence, Results of student A are both precise and accurate.

questions.question:
Consider the unbalanced chemical equation ,

-- Al + -- $O_2$ → -- $Al_2O_3$ .

The equation for the above reaction can be balanced by inserting the following in blank spaces.
questions.question_html:
Consider the unbalanced  chemical equation ,
-- Al + -- {tex}O_2{/tex} → -- {tex}Al_2O_3{/tex}.
The equation for the above reaction can be balanced by inserting the following in blank spaces.
questions.explanation:
The unbalanced chemical reaction is 

Al(s) + O_(2)​(g) $\longrightarrow$ Al_(2)​O_(3)​(s)

Balance Al atoms by adding 2 to Al on LHS

2Al(s) + O_(2)​(g) $\longrightarrow$ Al_(2)​O_(3)​(s)

Balance O atoms by adding $\frac 32$ ​ to O_(2)​ on LHS

2Al(s) + ​ $\frac 32$ O_(2)​(g) $\longrightarrow$ Al_(2)​O_(3)​(s)

Multiply the whole equation with 2.

4Al(s) + 3O_(2)​(g) $\longrightarrow$ 2Al_(2)​O_(3)​(s)

questions.question:

Identify the element(s) that exhibits only a Positive oxidation state?
questions.question_html:
Consider the elements: Cs, Ne, I, and F.
Identify the element(s) that exhibits only a Positive oxidation state?
questions.explanation: Cs is alkali metal so will show a positive oxidation state of +1.

questions.question: The bond formed, as a result of the electrostatic attraction between the positive and negative ions was termed as:
questions.question_html: The bond formed, as a result of the electrostatic attraction between the positive and negative ions was termed as:
questions.explanation: The chemical bond formed between two atoms due to the transfer of electron(s) from one atom to the other. atom is called an "ionic bond" or "electrovalent bond". In an electrovalent bond one atom loses an electron to form a positive ion and the other atom gains the electron to form a negative ion. The resulting ions are held together by electrostatic attraction.

questions.question: Hybridisation in methane (CH_(4)) ethene (C_(2)H_(4)) ethyne (C_(2)H_(2)) involves s and p orbitals. Choose the correct hybrid orbitals in the options given below for CH_(4),_( )C_(2)H_(4) and C_(2)H_(2) respectively:
questions.question_html: Hybridisation in methane (CH4) ethene (C2H4) ethyne (C2H2) involves s and p orbitals. Choose the correct hybrid orbitals in the options given below for CH4, C2H4 and C2H2 respectively:
questions.explanation: In methane C is sp^(3) hybridised because of 3 sigma bond formation. While in ethane both the C are sp^(2) hybridised, and in ethyne both the C are sp hybridised.

questions.question: The molar mass of Al_(2)O_(3) is:
questions.question_html: The molar mass of Al2O3 is:
questions.explanation:
Molar mass

= $\Sigma$ [ 2 $\times$ (atomic mass of Al) 3 $\times$ (atomic mass of O)]u

= [(2 $\times$ 27) + (3 $\times$ 16)]u

= ( 54 + 48 ) u

= 102 u.

questions.question:
Complete the statement with suitable word /(s)

Modern Periodic Law is stated as:

The physical and chemical properties of different elements are periodic functions of their ______.
questions.question_html:
Complete the statement with suitable  word /(s)
Modern Periodic Law is stated as:
The physical and chemical properties of different elements are periodic functions of their ______.
questions.explanation:
The Modern periodic law is stated as:

The physical and chemical properties of elements are periodic functions of their atomic numbers. That is the properties of elements will be repeated after regular intervals.

questions.question: In ${\text{PO}}_4^{3 - }$ ion the formal charge on the oxygen atom of P–O bond is
questions.question_html: In {tex}{\text{PO}}_4^{3 - }{/tex} ion the formal charge on the oxygen atom of P–O bond is
questions.explanation:
Formal change = $\frac{Total \ charge}{no.\ of\ o-atom}$ 

= $\frac {-3}4$ = -0.75

questions.question: H_(2)O_(2) as an antiseptic is used in the name of ________.
questions.question_html: H2O2 as an antiseptic is used in the name of ________.
questions.explanation: Perhydrol is a solution of hydrogen peroxide (H_(2)O_(2)) at a concentration of 30% of weight by volume(w/v) or 100 volume strength. Perhydrol is a mild antiseptic used on the skin to prevent infection of minor cuts, scrapes, and burns. It may also be used as a mouth rinse to help remove mucus or to relieve minor mouth irritation (e.g., due to canker/ cold sores, gingivitis). This product works by releasing oxygen when it is applied to the affected area. The release of oxygen causes foaming, which helps to remove dead skin and clean the area.

questions.question: Change in hybridisation affects the carbon’s and the organic compound’s:
questions.question_html: Change in hybridisation affects the carbon’s and the organic compound’s:
questions.explanation:
In hybridization as % of 's' character increases electronegativity also increases.

More the s character more will be the electronegativity of that atom.

Increasing order of electronegativity with %s character is sp (50%) > sp^(2)(33.33%) > sp^(3)(25%).

questions.question: Rutherford’s $\alpha$ –particle scattering experiment supported the discovery of:
questions.question_html: Rutherford’s {tex}\alpha{/tex}–particle scattering experiment supported the discovery of:
questions.explanation:
Rutherford's model of an atom: Ernest Rutherford was interested in knowing how the electrons are arranged within an atom. Rutherford designed an experiment for this. In this experiment, fast-moving alpha ( $\alpha$ )-particles were made to fall on a thin gold foil.

On the basis of his experiment, Rutherford put forward the model of an atom, which had the following features:

 There is a positively charged centre in an atom called the nucleus.
 Nearly all the mass of an atom resides in the nucleus. The electrons revolve around the nucleus in well-defined orbits.
 The size of the nucleus is very small as compared to the size of the atom.

questions.question: What would be the molarity of NaOH in a solution prepared by dissolving its 4 g in enough water to form 250 mL of the solution?
questions.question_html: What would be the molarity of NaOH in a solution prepared by dissolving its 4 g in enough water to form 250 mL of the solution?
questions.explanation:
Given that 250 ml of solution contains 4 g NaOH

 $\therefore$ Number of moles of NaOH in 250 ml of the prepared solution = (4/40) 

= 0.1 moles 

& number of moles of NaOH per litre of this solution 

= 0.4 moles

Since Molarity (M)= number of moles of solute present in 1 Litre of the solution,

Therefore, the Molarity of NaOH solution is 0.4 M.

questions.question: The hybridization of orbitals of N atom in ${\text{NO}}_3^ - $ , ${\text{NO}}_2^ + $ and $NH_4^ + $ are respectively:

questions.question: Write the state of hybridisation of carbon in H_(2)C = O and mention the molecular geometry also.
questions.question_html: Write the state of hybridisation of carbon in H2C = O and mention the molecular geometry also.
questions.explanation: Due to the presence of a double bond in the carbonyl carbon ( $>\mathrm C=\mathrm O$ ), carbon is sp^(2) hybridised. This hybridization leads to a trigonal planar geometry which means a bond angle of 120° around the C.

questions.question: Energy of an electron in stationary state is given by the formula ${{\text{E}}_{{\text{n}} = }}{{\text{R}}_{\text{H}}}\left\{ {1\frac{1}{{{n^2}}}} \right\}$ where n=1,2,3....... . In this equation, R_(H) is called ________.

questions.explanation: The Rydberg constant, symbol $R_\infty$ for heavy atoms or R_(H) for hydrogen, named after the Swedish physicist Johannes Rydberg, is a physical constant relating to atomic spectra, in the science of spectroscopy.

questions.question:
Assertion: Absorption spectrum consists of some bright lines separated by dark spaces.

Reason: Emission spectrum consists of dark lines.
questions.question_html:
Assertion: Absorption spectrum consists of some bright lines separated by dark spaces.
Reason: Emission spectrum consists of dark lines.

options.text: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
options.html: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.html: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

options.text: A is true but R is false.
options.html: A is true but R is false.

options.text: If both Assertion and Reason are false statements.
options.html: If both Assertion and Reason are false statements.

correct_options: If both Assertion and Reason are false statements.
questions.explanation: The absorption spectrum is produced when white light is passed through a substance and transmitted light is analyzed by a spectrograph. The dark spaces correspond to the light radiation absorbed by the substance. And the emission spectrum is produced by analyzing the radiant energy emitted by an excited substance by a spectrograph. Thus, discontinuous spectra consisting of a series of sharp lines and separated by dark bands are obtained.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Chemical reactivity is high at the two extremes and the lowest in the centre.

Reason (R): The ionization enthalpy of the extreme left element in a period is the least and the electron gain enthalpy of the element on the extreme right is the highest negative.
questions.question_html:
Assertion (A): Chemical reactivity is high at the two extremes and the lowest in the centre.
Reason (R): The ionization enthalpy of the extreme left element in a period is the least and the electron gain enthalpy of the element on the extreme right is the highest negative.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.html: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

options.text: A is true but R is false.
options.html: A is true but R is false.

options.text: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
options.html: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

options.text: A is false but R is true.
options.html: A is false but R is true.

correct_options: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: Chemical reactivity is high at the two extremes and the lowest in the centre. The maximum chemical reactivity at the extreme left (among alkali metals) is exhibited by the loss of an electron leading to the formation of a cation and at the extreme right (among halogens) shown by the gain of an electron forming an anion.

questions.explanation: Hess's law states that the enthalpy of a reaction is the same, whether it takes place in a single step or in more than one step. In the Born Haber cycle the formation of an ionic compound may occur either by direct combination of the elements or by a stepwise process involving vaporization of elements, conversion of the gaseous atoms into ions and the combination of the gaseous ions to form the ionic solid.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): The decomposition of hydrogen peroxide to form water and oxygen is an example of a disproportionation reaction.

Reason (R): The oxygen of peroxide is in -1 oxidation state and it is converted to zero oxidation state in O_(2 )and -2 oxidation state in H_(2)O.

questions.question: -122 °C in Fahrenheit scale is
questions.question_html: -122 °C in Fahrenheit scale is
questions.explanation:
°F = [ (°C $\times$ 9/5 ) + 32 ]

Substituting the given temperature in ^(o) C we get,

 $\therefore$ ^( o)F = [( -122°C $\times$ 1.8 ) + 32]

= ( -219.6 + 32 )

-122°C = - 187.6 °F

questions.question: First element in periodic table to have 8 valence electrons is
questions.question_html: First element in periodic table to have 8 valence electrons is

questions.question: In general, in the molecules containing multiple bonds, ________ provide the most reactive centres. Choose the most appropriate one.
questions.question_html: In general, in the molecules containing multiple bonds, ________ provide the most reactive centres. Choose the most appropriate one.
questions.explanation: $\pi$ bond formed by sideways overlapping of p orbitals/d orbitals on adjacent atoms provides the most reactive centers.

questions.question:
Instructions for Question 53 to 55:
In 1830, Michael Faraday showed that if electricity is passed through a solution of an electrolyte, chemical reactions occurred at the electrodes, which resulted in the liberation and deposition of matter at the electrodes. In the mid-1850s Faraday began to study electrical discharge in partially evacuated tubes, known as cathode ray discharge tubes. When sufficiently high voltage is applied across the electrodes, current starts flowing through a stream of particles moving in the tube from the negative electrode to the positive electrode. These were called cathode rays or cathode ray particles. J.J. Thomson measured the ratio of electrical charge (e) to the mass of the electron (m_(e)) by using a cathode ray tube and applying electrical and magnetic fields perpendicular to each other as well as to the path of electrons. Positively charged particle was characterised in 1919. Later, a need was felt for the presence of electrically neutral particles as one of the constituents of the atom.

Assertion (A): The cathode rays start from cathode and move towards the anode.

Reason (R): In the absence of an electrical or magnetic field, cathode rays travel in straight lines.
questions.question_html:
Instructions for Question 53 to 55:

In 1830, Michael Faraday showed that if electricity is passed through a solution of an electrolyte, chemical reactions occurred at the electrodes, which resulted in the liberation and deposition of matter at the electrodes. In the mid-1850s Faraday began to study electrical discharge in partially evacuated tubes, known as cathode ray discharge tubes.  When sufficiently high voltage is applied across the electrodes, current starts flowing through a stream of particles moving in the tube from the negative electrode to the positive electrode. These were called cathode rays or cathode ray particles. J.J. Thomson measured the ratio of electrical charge (e) to the mass of the electron (me) by using a cathode ray tube and applying electrical and magnetic fields perpendicular to each other as well as to the path of electrons. Positively charged particle was characterised in 1919. Later, a need was felt for the presence of electrically neutral particles as one of the constituents of the atom.


Assertion (A): The cathode rays start from cathode and move towards the anode.
Reason (R): In the absence of an electrical or magnetic field, cathode rays travel in straight lines.

questions.question:
Instructions for Question 53 to 55:
In 1830, Michael Faraday showed that if electricity is passed through a solution of an electrolyte, chemical reactions occurred at the electrodes, which resulted in the liberation and deposition of matter at the electrodes. In the mid-1850s Faraday began to study electrical discharge in partially evacuated tubes, known as cathode ray discharge tubes. When sufficiently high voltage is applied across the electrodes, current starts flowing through a stream of particles moving in the tube from the negative electrode to the positive electrode. These were called cathode rays or cathode ray particles. J.J. Thomson measured the ratio of electrical charge (e) to the mass of the electron (m_(e)) by using a cathode ray tube and applying electrical and magnetic fields perpendicular to each other as well as to the path of electrons. Positively charged particle was characterised in 1919. Later, a need was felt for the presence of electrically neutral particles as one of the constituents of the atom.

Assertion (A): Thomas argued that the lighter the particle, greater the deflection.

Reason (R): Deflection depends upon the mass of the particle.

questions.question:
Instructions for Question 53 to 55:
In 1830, Michael Faraday showed that if electricity is passed through a solution of an electrolyte, chemical reactions occurred at the electrodes, which resulted in the liberation and deposition of matter at the electrodes. In the mid-1850s Faraday began to study electrical discharge in partially evacuated tubes, known as cathode ray discharge tubes. When sufficiently high voltage is applied across the electrodes, current starts flowing through a stream of particles moving in the tube from the negative electrode to the positive electrode. These were called cathode rays or cathode ray particles. J.J. Thomson measured the ratio of electrical charge (e) to the mass of the electron (m_(e)) by using a cathode ray tube and applying electrical and magnetic fields perpendicular to each other as well as to the path of electrons. Positively charged particle was characterised in 1919. Later, a need was felt for the presence of electrically neutral particles as one of the constituents of the atom.

Assertion (A): Television picture tubes are anode ray tubes.

Reason (R): Electrons are the basic constituent of all atoms.
